name: Middle Irish (900-1200)

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Middle Irish (10-12th century) (midd1360).

notes: Skeleton. Goidelic (Insular Celtic) medieval stage written in the Latin alphabet. Grapheme map covers the basic vowels (with the acute marking length), the plain consonant letters, and the lenition digraphs ch/th/ph. The pervasive broad/slender (velarised vs palatalised) consonant contrast is not orthographically recoverable and is left unencoded; consonant lenition was written inconsistently in the manuscripts and only the h-digraphs are captured. Reconstructed phonetic values carry appreciable uncertainty, so a substantial PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Middle Irish (10-12th century) (midd1360)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
